notes: Both tables lint 1:1 against FIDELITY.md; FIDELITY_MATRIX.md stays a legacy pinned matrix. The refunds chain is implemented but undocumented — declared in doc_drift until F-733 reconciles the docs. The customer-management chain (F-732) carries its ruled heat (hot, F-729); the remaining pre-M5 surfaces stay unclassified until their owning tickets (F-731 payments, F-733 refunds docs, F-734 warm set) land.

tools.name: create_customer
tools.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729): customer management is the precondition of nearly every collection chain. Vendor MCP has meta-tools only; task chain wins per rubric.

tools.name: retrieve_customer
tools.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729).

tools.name: update_customer
tools.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729); Stripe per-key metadata merge modeled semantically.

tools.name: delete_customer
tools.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729); soft delete serves the {deleted: true} stub and detaches PMs like real Stripe.

tools.name: list_customers
tools.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729); cursor pagination + email filter.

tools.name: list_customer_payment_methods
tools.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729): the customer-scoped PM list is the hot read; the top-level PM list stays cold.

tools.name: create_payment_method
tools.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729): card PMs from test card numbers; PAN never stored. Card-mode PI confirm lands in F-731.

tools.name: retrieve_payment_method
tools.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729).

tools.name: attach_payment_method
tools.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729): one customer per PM; a detached PM can never be reattached (Stripe lifecycle rule).

tools.name: detach_payment_method
tools.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729).

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/customers
rest.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729): customer management is the precondition of nearly every collection chain.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/customers/:id
rest.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729); deleted customers serve the {deleted: true} stub like real Stripe.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/customers
rest.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729); cursor pagination + email filter, deleted rows excluded.

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/customers/:id
rest.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729); Stripe per-key metadata merge (empty value unsets).

rest.name: DELETE /s/:sid/v1/customers/:id
rest.justification: TC:customer-mgmt (ruled F-729); soft delete detaches the customer's PMs in the same transaction.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/customers/:id/payment_methods
rest.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729): the customer-scoped PM list is the hot read; the top-level GET /v1/payment_methods stays cold (loud 501).

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/payment_methods
rest.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729): card PMs from test card numbers (Luhn + expiry card_errors); PAN never stored.

rest.name: GET /s/:sid/v1/payment_methods/:id
rest.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729).

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/payment_methods/:id/attach
rest.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729): one customer per PM; a detached PM can never be reattached.

rest.name: POST /s/:sid/v1/payment_methods/:id/detach
rest.justification: TC:card-on-file (ruled F-729).
